skill.name: when-creating-presentations-use-pptx-generation
skill.description: Generate structured PowerPoint presentations with repeatable slide planning, design constraints, accessibility checks, and PPTX export steps.
skill.summary: Create professional PowerPoint decks with structured content, consistent layouts, charts, notes, and accessibility review.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak cryptography alerts, but review found they are false positives from presentation terminology and Graphviz labels, not crypto code. The confirmed risks are moderate: the README instructs users to run an external npx package, and the skill examples include local file writes for generated reports. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: The README instructs users to run an npx command for claude-flow@alpha. This is legitimate for a workflow skill, but it can execute code from an external package source and creates supply-chain risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external command is explicit in README.md and the dependency list references claude-flow@alpha. The context indicates intended execution, not malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Write for Generated Reports
medium_findings.description: The workflow example writes an accessibility report based on the output filename. This is expected for a PPTX generation skill, but users should constrain output paths to avoid unintended file writes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fs.writeFile call is directly present and writes a generated JSON report. The surrounding context is ordinary export logic with no evidence of destructive writes or data theft.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts map to presentation words, slide design labels, Graphviz node labels, and JavaScript examples. No hashing, encryption, weak cipher selection, or password storage logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain documentation and presentation workflow text at the cited locations. There is no cryptographic API usage or semantic crypto behavior.

low_findings.title: Code Fence Backtick Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts occur in Markdown code fences and template examples, not Ruby execution contexts. They do not create command execution by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are Markdown delimiters and JavaScript template literals inside documentation. No Ruby interpreter or shell backtick execution path is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Execution Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks users to run an npx command that may fetch and execute an external alpha package. This pattern is acceptable with disclosure and dependency review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command line is visible and directly executable. Risk depends on the external package integrity, not on hidden behavior in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated File Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes an fs.writeFile example for writing an accessibility report. The behavior is normal for document generation but should run in a user-approved output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem write is directly present. The context is report generation, and no evidence found of unauthorized file access.

content.value_statement: Teams often need polished presentations but lose time on layout, charts, and accessibility checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a repeatable PPTX workflow from outline to export.
actual_capabilities: Structures presentation metadata, outline sections, slide types, and data points before slide generation.
Defines reusable slide layouts for title, content, two-column, and chart-based slides.
Uses pptxgenjs examples to generate slides, add text, add charts, and export a PPTX file.
Includes accessibility checks for contrast, alt text, reading order, and WCAG 2.1 AA targets.
Produces companion artifacts such as accessibility reports, presentation documentation, and speaker notes.
limitations: Requires appropriate JavaScript tooling and presentation dependencies to produce real PPTX files.
Does not verify factual accuracy of source content without separate research and review.
Accessibility checks are workflow guidance and example logic, not a certified compliance audit.
External package execution should be reviewed before use in restricted environments.

use_cases.title: Prepare Executive Board Decks
use_cases.description: Create a structured presentation with an executive summary, key data visuals, recommendations, and speaker notes.

use_cases.title: Turn Technical Reports Into Slides
use_cases.description: Convert dense technical material into readable slides with consistent layouts, charts, and accessibility checkpoints.

use_cases.title: Standardize Client Proposal Decks
use_cases.description: Build proposal decks from reusable sections while preserving brand colors, slide structure, and export documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a short presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PPTX generation skill to create a 10-slide presentation from this outline. Include title, agenda, summary, content slides, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Review Deck
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a quarterly business review deck from these metrics. Choose chart layouts, add speaker notes, and include an accessibility checklist.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Design System
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for branded presentation work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a PowerPoint deck using these brand colors, fonts, and layout rules. Validate contrast, reading order, and slide consistency before export.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Complete Client Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for deliverables and documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a client proposal deck with charts, recommendations, speaker notes, an accessibility report, and generation documentation for review.

output_examples.input: A product launch outline with audience, goals, key messages, and launch metrics.
output_examples.output: A PowerPoint deck with title, agenda, market context, launch plan, metric charts, risks, recommendations, and speaker notes.

output_examples.input: A quarterly business review with revenue, adoption, churn, and customer feedback data.
output_examples.output: A business review deck with executive summary, trend charts, insight slides, action items, and an accessibility report.

output_examples.input: A technical architecture report for a mixed executive and engineering audience.
output_examples.output: A presentation with overview slides, simplified architecture diagrams, technical appendix slides, and clear reading order guidance.

best_practices: Provide a complete outline, audience, goal, and source data before generating slides.
Review all external dependencies and run commands only in a trusted project environment.
Validate facts, chart values, contrast, alt text, and speaker notes before sharing the deck.
anti_patterns: Do not generate a deck from vague goals without source material or audience details.
Do not run the npx command in a sensitive environment without reviewing the package first.
Do not treat automated accessibility checks as a substitute for human review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It guides creation of PowerPoint presentations, accessibility reports, documentation, and speaker notes.

faq.question: Does it generate real .pptx files?
faq.answer: Yes, when the required JavaScript dependencies and runtime environment are available.

faq.question: Can it build charts?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes chart layout selection and pptxgenjs chart examples.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for external command execution and local file writes.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility support?
faq.answer: Yes. It targets contrast checks, alt text, reading order, and WCAG 2.1 AA guidance.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Use it for business reviews, board decks, client proposals, technical reports, and training presentations.
